lore__the_squishing.title: Lore: The Squishing
lore__the_squishing.description:
[From the Mages Guild Archives, transcribed by Brother Matthias]
2003. That's when everything broke. Not metaphorically—literally. Reality itself got compressed like a tin can under a boot. Ninety-two dimensions, all distinct, all separate, all with their own laws... crushed together into one screaming mess.
Physics doesn't work right anymore. Time flows backwards in some places, sideways in others. I've seen space fold itself into shapes that make your eyes bleed. The boundary between what's possible and what isn't? Gone. Shattered. There is no boundary now.
What we're left with is this patchwork existence—fragments of ninety-two different realities all jammed together, grinding against each other like broken gears. The old dimensions? They exist as 'Scraps' now, floating islands suspended over the Abyss. Don't ask me what the Abyss is. I've met three people who went down there. Two came back wrong. One didn't come back at all.
It started with Y2K in '99. Everyone thought the Millennium Bug would crash some computers. Instead it broke technology itself. The lights went out and never really came back on. That's when we had to remember the old ways. Magic. Ritual. Blood.
Now we live in the ruins, where a Roman amphitheater might lead into a Victorian factory that opens onto prehistoric marshland. The Omega Tower holds it all together—barely. Without it, reality would probably just... collapse entirely.
Stay alert. Geography lies here.

lore__the_rule_of_80.title: Lore: The Rule of 80
lore__the_rule_of_80.description:
[Warning carved into the dashboard of every Trucker rig]
Stay fast or stay forgotten.
80 kilometers per hour. That's the line. Go slower and you're caught in the loop—twenty-four hours that repeat forever. Die below the threshold and you'll wake up the next morning like nothing happened. Sounds good? It's not. You won't remember dying. You won't remember yesterday. You won't remember anything.
I've talked to loopers. Every damn day they're surprised the calendar's wrong. Every day they make the same mistakes. They're stuck in amber, living the same day over and over, convinced each time it's new.
Above 80? Different story. You escape the reset. Time moves forward. Memories stick. But die at speed and you're gone for real. No loops. No second chances. Just dead.
The Hypercapitalists figured this out early. Bio-modded themselves into speedfreaks who literally can't stop moving. Extra legs, wheeled feet, bodies that feed on velocity. While everyone else resets and forgets, they remember everything. They've been accumulating wealth and power for two decades while the rest of us keep losing our memories.
That's how they control everything now. They move too fast to reset, and they've got time on their side—literal, actual time that everyone else keeps losing.
Keep moving. Don't settle down. Don't get comfortable. Once you drop below 80 and start looping, you might never realize you're trapped.

lore__the_encompassing_road.title: Lore: The Encompassing Road
lore__the_encompassing_road.description:
[From the Truckers Society Handbook, Chapter 1: Don't Die Stupid]
The Asphalt God is dead. When it fell, the Road was born.
Forget everything about highways. The Road isn't pavement—it's alive. It thinks. It wants things. Take Exit 47 and you might end up next Tuesday. Take Exit 48 and you're in the Cretaceous. I once saw a ramp that looped through seventeen different dimensions before spitting us out in medieval Prague.
The Road connects everything now. It's the only thing holding the shattered realities together, which is both convenient and terrifying.
Dangers? Where do I start. Time Loops aren't just annoying—they're predatory. Road Shadows manifest your existential dread and then try to eat you. Dimensional Rifts tear open without warning. I've seen drivers age forty years in four hours because of Temporal Distortions.
The Truckers Society exists because someone has to navigate this nightmare. We're not heroes. We're just the idiots crazy enough to drive between dimensions for a living. Our rigs have Anoki phones welded into the dashboard—only things tough enough to survive temporal anomalies. They're also the only phones that still work.
Rest stops are currency now. Some are just burned-out campgrounds. Others are interdimensional bazaars selling artifacts from futures that haven't happened yet. Information's worth more than gold out here. In certain rest stops, you can literally buy and sell time.
Rule one: trust your navigator. Rule two: never stop moving. Rule three: if the Road starts whispering, drive faster.

lore__the_mages_guild.title: Lore: The Mages Guild
lore__the_mages_guild.description:
[Overheard in the Guild halls - speaker unknown]
We used to be the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn. Secret. Powerful. Then '99 and '03 happened and secrecy became pointless. Hard to hide when reality's breaking apart and you're the only ones who know why.
Problem is, we can't agree on anything. We've got more magic than anyone, more resources, more knowledge—and we spend it all fighting each other.
The Thatcher Loyalists want control. Everything regulated, licensed, monitored. They see magic like nuclear weapons—too dangerous for freedom. They're the ones who did the Falklands ritual, who think order matters more than ethics. Lady Margaret's ghost would be proud.
Crowley's followers, on the other hand, think we're all fascists. They want wild magic, personal enlightenment, transformation through chaos. They warned us about the Solomon Ritual. Warned us about ripping holes between dimensions. Nobody listened.
Then there's the Technomancers. Newest faction, weirdest ideas. They're trying to compile magic like code, treating spells as software. Their SpellForge Engine actually works, which terrifies the traditionalists.
The 92 Schools Branch just tries to document everything. Poor bastards. They're cataloging magic from ninety-two merged dimensions while we're busy arguing about whose philosophy is right.
We've got an enforcement division too, but half the time they're arresting each other for ideological crimes. Last week they tried to detain a Crowley ritualist for unsanctioned dimension-walking. He turned the enforcer into a toad. Guild politics at its finest.
We could fix so much if we'd stop fighting ourselves. We won't, though. That's not how mages work.

lore__hypercapitalist_collective.title: Lore: Hypercapitalist Collective
lore__hypercapitalist_collective.description:
[Graffiti found in abandoned subway station]
They don't walk. They don't stop. They barely look human anymore.
The Hypercapitalists took the Rule of 80 and made it their religion. Bio-modded themselves into things that feed on speed. I've seen them—extra limbs sprouting from torsos, legs that end in wheels, eyes that track trajectories faster than thought. They move like insects, twitching, never still.
They CAN'T stop. Their metabolism burns velocity as fuel. Stand still and they starve. That's not metaphor—they literally eat momentum.
It's all pyramid scheme. Want to rise in the ranks? Move faster. Close bigger deals. Never. Stop. Running. The Lords at the top are blurs, moving so fast they're practically ghosts. They've been awake and remembering for twenty years while everyone else resets daily.
Worst part? The water. They control all of it. Every clean source. Everything else is spiked with LSD—for them it's performance enhancing, makes them faster, sharper. For the rest of us? Hallucinations. Confusion. Can't organize resistance when you can't trust your own eyes.
They remember everything. Every deal. Every debt. Every slight. They've been compounding advantage for two decades while normal people keep forgetting they were screwed yesterday. It's not just wealth anymore—they own time itself.
I've seen them do business across dimensions. Mining parallel realities. Treating the entire multiverse like their corporate territory. Nothing's sacred. Nothing's safe. Everything has a price and they're the only ones who can afford to remember what they paid.
Stay human. Stay slow if you have to. Once you start running with them, you won't remember how to stop.

lore__em,_the_memory_witch.title: Lore: Em, the Memory Witch
lore__em,_the_memory_witch.description:
[Fragment recovered from Ritual Island, author unclear]
She doesn't remember her name. Calls herself Em because that's what someone once called her, maybe. She's not sure.
I found records. She was young, obsessed with magic, recruited by Thatcher for the Solomon Ritual—the big one, the working that severed divinity from mortality. To make it work, she had to become the focal point. The lens. The sacrifice.
Ninety-two percent of her memories. Gone. Alchemically transmuted into the Spear of Memory. That's the weapon Eris used to kill God. Em's forgotten childhood, her first love, her mother's face—all forged into decide.
January 1st, 2001. She woke up alone on the island with power that could crack dimensions and absolutely no idea who she was. The Guild left her there. Job done. Purpose fulfilled. She was the tool, and tools get discarded.
Now she wanders. Looking for something. Herself, maybe. Pieces of what she lost. Some say her memories are scattered across all ninety-two dimensions like shrapnel. Others think she's trying to undo the Solomon Ritual, to put reality back the way it was.
People who meet her say she's wrong. Ancient and infant simultaneously. Devastating power in something that looks lost. She'll help you sometimes, for reasons she herself doesn't understand. Like muscle memory from a person who doesn't exist anymore.
One theory terrifies me: she's rebuilding herself into something new. Something that touched divinity without becoming divine. Something that was never supposed to exist.
If you see her, be kind. She gave everything to break the world. Least we can do is not make it worse.

lore__camelcase_magic.title: Lore: CamelCase Magic
lore__camelcase_magic.description:
[Warning posted in every Guild apprentice dormitory]
LISTEN. This will save your life, your voice, and your existence.
After the Squishing, magic broke and reformed under new rules. The biggest rule: CamelCase formatting. Not negotiable. Not optional. MANDATORY.
FireBall. IceShield. LightningStrike. Words mashed together, capitals marking new words. That's how spells work now. That's ALL that works.
'Fire ball' will rip your vocal cords out. 'fireball' will deteragrammatize you—that means erased from every written record, like you never existed. Even infected written spells spread corruption to nearby texts.
Multi-word spells? Transcendental incantations like Create Universe, Unmake Death, Rewrite History? Those are for gods only. Mortals who try don't just die. They experience things worse than death. Things that don't have words.
Why? Nobody knows for sure. Best theory: reality's error-checking after ninety-two dimensions got compressed into one. The universe debugging itself. Some think it's intentional—a constraint by whatever force now governs magic. Others say it's just how broken reality behaves.
The Technomancers love it. Treat magic like code, compile spells from CamelCase components. Their SpellForge Engine actually works because they embraced the limitation.
I watched an apprentice try to cast 'fire bolt' last week. Said it was 'more natural.' We scraped what was left of her off the walls. There was less than you'd think.
Learn. The Format. Or. Die.
No exceptions. No mercy. Reality doesn't care about your preferences.

lore__alagadda,_city_of_masks.title: Lore: Alagadda, City of Masks
lore__alagadda,_city_of_masks.description:
[Found scrawled on paper, ink smudged with tears]
Don't go there. Don't even look for it. Alagadda finds you, not the other way around.
It exists between dimensions. You access it through threshold moments—a masquerade where the dancers realize their masks won't come off and the walls start melting. Mirrors reflecting places that don't exist. Doors appearing where walls stood seconds ago. These aren't invitations. They're traps.
FIRST RULE: MASKS STAY ON. ALWAYS.
Take your mask off for one second and you become Unperson. Invisible. Intangible. Unable to die, unable to interact, unable to be seen. You'll drift through that city forever, screaming at people who can't hear you, touching walls that don't feel you, existing in a hell that forgot you're real.
Time is broken there. I spent what felt like three days. Outside, six months had passed. Someone else stayed for years—came out two minutes later. The city doesn't follow rules. It makes them up as it goes, and it hates you.
The Palace of Eyes—don't look at the walls, they look back. The Bridge of Lament whispers your secrets, your failures, everything you regret. The Masquerade Markets sell anything. Anything. Memories. Emotions. Time. Souls. The prices are always worse than what you're buying.
Hazards everywhere. The Maskless are empty things hunting for faces. The Plague of Joy makes you laugh until you come apart. Living Masks attach to your face and start changing you from the inside out.
I got out. Barely. I'm not the same. Something's different now, something I can't name. I look in mirrors and sometimes I'm not there.
If you find yourself in Alagadda, remember: trust nothing, touch nothing, speak to no one, and NEVER. REMOVE. YOUR. MASK.

blade_seeds.title: Blade Seeds
blade_seeds.description:
In the scattered remains of the old world, few artifacts carry as much significance as the Blade Seeds. These mystical seeds allow travelers to forge a spiritual weapon—a companion that will grow alongside them through their journey across the fractured lands of Europe.
When binding a Blade Seed, you must first choose the form your weapon will take. The seed responds to your nature, manifesting as a blade, bow, staff, or other instrument of your profession. Upon binding, the seed generates a unique name for your weapon and awakens a spirit guardian from within—each with its own personality, elemental affinity, and strength.
Your bonded spirit absorbs half of every lesson you learn, growing more powerful as you do. Its essence merges with yours, its strength becoming your strength. At significant milestones in your development, the spirit will evolve, transforming into more magnificent and powerful forms.
With each strike you make, you earn Learning Points—spiritual insights that allow you to teach your guardian new abilities. Fire spirits master the art of flame, ice spirits command winter's bite, and so forth. More complex techniques require greater insight to master.
Be warned: once a Blade Seed is bound, the weapon becomes part of your very soul. It cannot be removed or replaced. This permanent bond, however, creates a partnership of unmatched power that will serve you faithfully through all trials ahead.

beginning_your_journey.title: Beginning Your Journey
beginning_your_journey.description:
Every traveler must choose their path before venturing into the strange new world that emerged after the Squishing. Your first decisions will shape everything that follows.
First, decide how you wish to face mortality. Some travelers embrace the old way—Permadeath, where death is final and requires beginning anew. You may recover possessions from your fallen body, but the journey must restart. Others prefer the Roguelite path, where defeat merely returns you to the base of the Omega Tower to try again, though companions lost in battle remain lost forever.
Next, consider your approach to conflict. Will you face adversaries through traditional combat methods, relying on strategic positioning and direct action? Or do you prefer the card-based tactical system, where battles become elaborate games of prediction and resource management? Choose also the music that will accompany your battles—eleven different compositions await, from drums to melodic techno.
You may either create yourself anew or adopt the identity of a known traveler. If forging your own path, you must declare:
- Your beliefs (Muscle, Science, Magic, Religion, Hypercapitalism, or Nothing—this shapes how certain locations respond to you)
- Your nature (Male, Female, Non-Binary, or Cocoon)
- Your physical form (chosen deliberately or left to chance)
- Your profession (64 distinct paths, each with unique strengths)
- Your defining traits
Each profession offers different aptitudes—some learn certain skills four times faster, others twice as fast. Each can wield different equipment and learns unique abilities as they grow stronger. Choose carefully, for your profession defines how you'll survive in this fractured world.

combat_system.title: Combat System
combat_system.description:
Combat in Hypernet Explorer is fast-paced and strategic, even though it's turn-based. You directly control only your main character, while party members are controlled by AI.
Enemies appear on the map and can be avoided or engaged. When you touch an enemy, battle begins. Enemy HP is persistent - if you flee from battle and return, enemies will still have reduced health.
You can target specific body parts on enemies to cripple them, reducing their stats. However, enemies can do the same to you! Losing limbs affects your combat effectiveness.
Each character has a one-time protection per battle: the first attack that would reduce you to 0 HP instead leaves you at 1 HP. This doesn't work if you're already at 1 HP, so use it wisely.
If your main character dies, the battle ends immediately. In Permadeath mode, this means creating a new character. In Roguelite mode, you respawn at the dungeon base.
If party members die and aren't revived before battle ends, they're lost forever and won't appear even for future characters. Protect your companions!
Escape is guaranteed on the first turn of any battle, giving you a chance to flee if overwhelmed.

cooking_system.title: Cooking System
cooking_system.description:
Cooking allows you to combine two healing items to create a more powerful item that affects your entire party.
Open the cooking menu from the main menu or at campfires. Select two food items or consumables from your inventory, and the system will combine them into a new item with enhanced effects.
Combining items is especially useful when you have many single-target healing items but need something that can heal or buff your whole party at once. Experiment with different combinations to discover powerful recipes!
Cooked items are consumed when used, just like regular items, but their party-wide effects make them invaluable during tough dungeon runs.

crafting_(thinker_system).title: Crafting (Thinker System)
crafting_(thinker_system).description:
The Thinker system becomes available when you possess certain special items. It lets you create new items from materials or break down existing items.
ASSEMBLE MODE:
Combine materials to craft new items. Items are organized by category (Food, Combat, Medical, etc.). You can only craft items if you have all required materials. Unknown recipes appear as '???' until you craft them once.
DISASSEMBLE MODE:
Break down items you already own to recover 1-2 random materials from their recipe. This is useful for recycling unwanted items into useful components.
Each item has a recipe defined by the materials needed. For example, a potion might require 3 herbs and 1 vial. Once you know a recipe, it will show you how many materials you have versus how many you need.
The Thinker menu shows counters for each category, displaying how many items are currently craftable. This helps you quickly see what you can make without searching through every category.

crime_&_law_system.title: Crime & Law System
crime_&_law_system.description:
Committing crimes will attract the attention of law enforcement. Each crime adds to your bounty, measured in gold (100 gold = 1 euro).
STEALING:
You can attempt to steal from shop shelves. Success depends on the item's value, weight, and your Dexterity stat. If caught, a police officer spawns at the shop entrance and your bounty increases based on the item's value.
POLICE ENCOUNTERS:
When police spot you with a bounty, they'll pursue you. If caught, you have three options:
- Pay your bounty in full
- Go to trial (outcome determined by your standing with Eris, goddess of discord)
- Serve time in prison
PRISON TIME:
In prison, your bounty decreases gradually over time (100 gold per second by default). You'll be released automatically when your bounty reaches zero.
TRIAL SYSTEM:
Trials involve procedural dialogue with Eris, who can be benevolent, vengeful, chaotic, playful, bored, dramatic, or irritated. Your choices during the trial influence the outcome. If found guilty, you go to prison. If found innocent, your bounty is cleared and you return to your previous location.
Be strategic about crime - sometimes it's worth the risk, but getting caught can derail your dungeon run!

dating_system.title: Dating System
dating_system.description:
You can develop a relationship with Eris, the goddess of discord and chaos.
Your first potential date occurs at the top of Ghent Station (Panorama Roof). If the date goes well, Eris will gift you a Blade Seed - one of the rarest items in the game.
After the first date, new date locations will unlock throughout the world. Your relationship is tracked by Eris's opinion value, which ranges from 0 to 1000. Higher values lead to better interactions.
Interestingly, your relationship with Eris also affects trial outcomes when you're caught committing crimes. A higher opinion means she's more likely to find you innocent or give you lighter sentences.
Build your relationship carefully - Eris is unpredictable by nature, but a strong bond with a goddess has incredible benefits!

death_&_gravestones.title: Death & Gravestones
death_&_gravestones.description:
Death works differently depending on your chosen difficulty mode.
ROGUELITE MODE:
When your main character dies, you respawn at the dungeon's base floor. You keep all your equipment but must climb back up through the floors you've already cleared.
PERMADEATH MODE:
Death means creating a new character. However, you don't lose everything:
WHAT'S SAVED:
- Your exact death location is marked
- All your gold is saved
- All unequipped items are saved
WHAT'S LOST:
- Equipped items are lost forever
- You lose levels (rounded down to previous multiple of 10)
THE GRAVESTONE:
When you return to where you died, you'll find a gravestone event. Interact with it to recover your gold and unequipped items. This only works once - if you die again, the new death overwrites the previous gravestone.
PARTY MEMBER DEATH:
If party members die in battle and aren't revived before the battle ends, they're permanently lost from that save file. They won't appear for any future characters either!
STOCKS & BANK:
Your stock market shares and bank account are inherited by new characters, so not all progress is lost.

dungeon_system.title: Dungeon System
dungeon_system.description:
The Omega Tower dungeon consists of 92 procedurally generated floors (30 in the demo version). Each floor is created using your character's name as a seed - different names produce different dungeon layouts, but the same name always generates the same dungeon.
The first floor is always fixed. After that, floors are randomly selected from pools of hand-crafted maps, with difficulty increasing as you climb higher.
Every 10 floors, you'll encounter special Elevator Floors with boss fights or unique events. These are the same for all players.
Floors can contain multiple rooms connected together. Entry and exit stairs will be placed in different rooms, requiring you to explore to find the way forward.
ENEMY SPAWNING:
Enemies spawn dynamically on each floor. Their levels are appropriate to your current floor, getting progressively harder as you climb. Enemies can move toward you, flee, or stand still depending on their behavior type.
LOOT:
Treasure chests are randomly placed on each floor, containing weapons, armor, and items. The quality of loot improves with higher floors.
SAVING:
You can save at taverns, using tents/sleeping bags, from the elevator, or on the world map. Save frequently - death can be sudden!
The dungeon is brutal and unforgiving. Use every advantage you can find to survive!

factions_&_reputation.title: Factions & Reputation
factions_&_reputation.description:
Various factions exist throughout the world, each with their own goals and philosophies. Your reputation with each faction ranges from -100 (hostile) to +100 (revered).
MAJOR FACTIONS:
The Mages Guild: Fractured magical organization divided between those who want control and order versus those who embrace chaos and freedom. They have the most magical power but internal conflicts weaken them.
The Hypercapitalist Collective: Ultra-capitalists who have bio-engineered themselves to thrive on constant movement. They control water supplies and trade routes between dimensions. Allied with the gods.
The Archive Foundation: Paramilitary organization dedicated to making all information public, no matter how sensitive. Will steal and leak anything from anyone.
The Gods: Led by Eris, goddess of discord, after she assassinated the previous divine leader. They maintain an alliance with the Hypercapitalists.
Goblin Factions: Two distinct goblin civilizations - the militant underground Naguka and the peaceful surface-dwelling Verden.
Your actions and choices affect your standing with these factions. High reputation unlocks unique quests, vendors, and storylines. Low reputation can make factions hostile or deny you access to their services.
Check your faction standings regularly to see how your choices have impacted the world!

fog_of_war.title: Fog of War
fog_of_war.description:
Many areas feature fog of war, which hides unexplored portions of the map. As you move through these areas, the fog gradually clears, revealing the terrain and any items or enemies present.
Certain regions increase your vision range, allowing you to see further through the fog. This encourages exploration and rewards players who carefully investigate their surroundings.
Fog of war adds tension to exploration - you never know what dangers lurk just beyond your vision! Be prepared for sudden enemy encounters when entering new areas.
Some maps disable fog of war entirely. Check your surroundings carefully in fogged areas before committing to a path.

hunger_&_sleep.title: Hunger & Sleep
hunger_&_sleep.description:
Survival isn't just about combat - you must also manage your character's basic needs.
HUNGER SYSTEM:
Each character has a hunger meter that starts at 100%. It decreases as you walk (0.05 per step). When running (holding Shift), hunger depletes 1.5x faster.
Hunger States:
- Above 20%: Normal condition
- Below 20%: Hungry - minor debuffs applied
- At 0%: Starving - severe debuffs and 1% HP loss per step
If HP reaches 0 from starvation, your character collapses.
SLEEP SYSTEM:
Sleep works similarly but depletes slower (0.03 per step, 1.5x when running).
Sleep States:
- Above 20%: Normal condition  
- Below 20%: Sleepy - minor debuffs applied
- At 0%: Exhausted - severe debuffs and 1% MP loss per step
MANAGING NEEDS:
Eat food items to restore hunger. Sleep at inns, campsites, or use sleeping bags to restore sleep. Both affect all party members when managed properly.
Plan your resources carefully during long dungeon runs - running out of food or rest can be just as deadly as enemies!

hypernet_&_shopping.title: Hypernet & Shopping
hypernet_&_shopping.description:
The Hypernet is this world's version of the internet, accessible through computers found at Hypernet Point and other locations.
ONLINE SHOPPING:
From any computer, you can access online shopping to purchase virtually any item or skill in the game. However, prices are significantly marked up compared to physical shops, and there's an additional tax based on SOUL share values.
NEWS:
Read the latest news to stay informed about world events. News stories affect the stock market and real estate values.
STOCK MARKET:
Trade shares directly from any computer. Monitor your investments and make trades without needing to visit a physical location.
REAL ESTATE:
Browse available properties and manage your real estate portfolio remotely.
The Hypernet represents the freedom of the early 2000s internet - a decentralized network where information flows freely. Many NPCs have their own personal websites you can visit.
Online shopping is convenient but expensive. Use it when you're desperate or can't find what you need in physical stores.

lockpicking.title: Lockpicking
lockpicking.description:
When you encounter locked doors or chests, you can attempt to pick the lock using a Tetris-style minigame.
The minigame functions like classic Tetris - falling blocks must be arranged to complete horizontal lines. Successfully clearing enough lines picks the lock.
Difficulty varies by location. Higher difficulty means:
- Faster falling blocks
- More complex block patterns  
- More lines required to succeed
If you succeed, the lock opens and you can access what's beyond. If you fail, you'll need to try again or find another route.
Lockpicking is a valuable skill for accessing treasure rooms, shortcuts, and secret areas throughout the dungeon and world. Practice your Tetris skills - they'll serve you well!

news_system.title: News System
news_system.description:
News events occur throughout the game, affecting the world's economy and politics. You can read news by using newspapers (item 588) or accessing computers.
NEWS EFFECTS:
News stories influence SOUL Tendency, a variable that affects:
- SOUL stock prices (negative news raises prices, positive news lowers them)
- Taxes on online purchases
- Real estate values in affected cities
Local news about specific cities directly impacts real estate prices in those locations. Pay attention to where events occur!
STRATEGIC USE:
Smart players can use news to their advantage:
- Buy SOUL shares before negative news breaks
- Invest in real estate in cities about to have positive news
- Time your online shopping to avoid high tax periods
News updates happen periodically, so check regularly to stay ahead of market changes. Information is power in the post-apocalyptic economy!

party_system.title: Party System
party_system.description:
You can recruit NPCs to join your party as you explore the world. Party members fight alongside you in battle and can be crucial to your survival in the dangerous dungeon.
RECRUITING:
Talk to NPCs throughout the world. Many are willing to join your cause. Each has their own class, skills, and personality.
PARTY MANAGEMENT:
You can have up to 3 party members active at once (including yourself). Party members gain experience and level up just like you do.
IMPORTANT WARNINGS:
- If a party member dies in battle and isn't revived before the battle ends, they're lost FOREVER
- This is permanent across all characters in that save file
- They won't appear for future playthroughs either
- Protect your companions or be ready to lose them
You can dismiss party members if you want to recruit someone else, but dismissed members can usually be found again later.
PARTY DYNAMICS:
Your party members have thoughts and opinions about events, battles, and each other. Check the Dynamics menu to see relationships between party members. These relationships may unlock special events in future updates.
Choose your companions wisely and keep them alive!

real_estate_market.title: Real Estate Market
real_estate_market.description:
You can invest in real estate by purchasing properties throughout the world. Access the real estate market through computers or bankers.
PROPERTY TYPES:
Various properties are available in different cities, each with different costs and rental income potential.
RENTAL INCOME:
Properties generate daily income from tenants. Access the "Check Daily Income" option to collect your rent. The amount depends on the property's value and location.
MARKET FLUCTUATIONS:
Property values change based on:
- SOUL stock market performance
- Local news events in that city
- Overall market conditions
You can buy low and sell high to profit from market swings, or hold properties long-term for steady rental income.
INHERITANCE:
Real estate investments persist between characters. If you die in Permadeath mode, your new character inherits all properties! This makes real estate one of the few permanent progression systems.
BUILDING WEALTH:
Smart real estate investments can provide passive income to fund your dungeon expeditions. Buy properties in stable cities for reliable income, or speculate on emerging markets for bigger profits.

saving_your_game.title: Saving Your Game
saving_your_game.description:
Saving is crucial in this challenging game. You can save your progress in several locations:
WORLD MAP:
You can always save when on the world map. This is the safest place to save.
TAVERNS:
Found at the dungeon's base and scattered throughout the world and dungeon. Rest at a tavern to save your game.
TENTS & SLEEPING BAGS:
Purchasable items that allow single-use saving. They're consumed when used, so buy them in bulk for long dungeon runs.
ELEVATOR:
Interact with the bonfire in the elevator room to save. This special save point is always available once you've unlocked the elevator.
SAVING STRATEGY:
Save frequently, especially:
- Before entering new dungeon floors
- After obtaining rare items or equipment  
- Before difficult boss battles
- After gaining several levels
Don't rely on finding a save point later - death can come suddenly in this game! The few moments spent saving could save you hours of lost progress.
Remember: In Permadeath mode, saving won't prevent death, but it will ensure you don't lose even more progress than necessary when starting a new character.

skills_&_training.title: Skills & Training
skills_&_training.description:
Skills are divided into categories, and each class has primary and secondary skill categories that receive learning bonuses.
LEARNING SKILLS:
You can learn new skills by:
- Leveling up (automatic for class skills)
- Finding skill scrolls in the world
- Purchasing from vendors
- Training at the Training Center
TRAINING SYSTEM:
Use the Training menu to actively train a skill. Training requires Knowledge Points earned through battles and exploration. Your class's primary skills train 4x faster, and secondary skills train 2x faster.
SKILL CATEGORIES:
Skills are organized into categories like Leadership, Roguery, Convocation, Natural Magic, Divination, Psychic Abilities, Void Magic, Law Enforcement, Martial Arts, and many more.
SKILL FUSION:
Once you've mastered multiple skills, you can combine them to create powerful hybrid abilities. Access the Skill Fusion menu to experiment with combinations.
SKILLOPEDIA:
Check the Skillopedia to view all skills in the game, their effects, requirements, and which classes can learn them easily.
Plan your skill development based on your playstyle and class. Specializing in your class's primary skills is efficient, but diversifying can give you tactical advantages!

scaling_&_attributes.title: Scaling & Attributes
scaling_&_attributes.description:
Damage scaling is how your abilities benefit from your character's attributes. Understanding scaling helps you optimize your equipment and skill selection.

\c[6]SKILL SCALING:\c[0]
Skills display a scale rating based on the formula of the ability:
- F Grade: No meaningful scaling or weak scaling (<1x multiplier)
- E Grade: Weak scaling (1-2x multiplier)
- D Grade: Low scaling (2-3x multiplier)
- C Grade: Medium scaling (3-5x multiplier)
- B Grade: Strong scaling (5-7x multiplier)
- A Grade: Very strong scaling (7-9x multiplier)
- S Grade: Extreme scaling (9x+ multiplier)

Scaling attributes include:
- STR (Strength): Physical power and melee damage
- DEX (Dexterity): Agility and precision strikes
- INT (Intelligence): Magical power and spell effectiveness
- CON (Constitution): Physical resilience and durability
- WIS/SAG (Wisdom): Magical defense and mental fortitude
- PSI (Psychic): Psychic abilities and esoteric powers
- MIX (Mixed): Hybrid scaling combining multiple attributes

\c[6]WEAPON SCALING:\c[0]
Weapons determine their scaling type based on attack skills equipped:
- No attack skill: STR scaling
- Attack Skill 840: DEX scaling (dexterous strikes)
- Attack Skill 841: MIX scaling (balanced approach)
- Attack Skill 842: PSI scaling (psychic-enhanced attacks)
- Attack Skill 843: INT scaling (spell-enhanced strikes)
- Attack Skill 844: CON scaling (durable-based hits)
- Attack Skill 845: WIS scaling (wisdom-enhanced attacks)

\c[6]MIX SCALING EXPLAINED:\c[0]
MIX scaling is a special hybrid mode that doesn't follow standard attribute formulas. Instead, it represents a balanced, adaptable approach that combines principles from multiple scaling types. This makes MIX weapons useful when you want flexibility or when your character has mixed attribute distributions rather than specializing in one stat.

\c[6]EQUIPMENT SYNERGY:\c[0]
Your equipped gear influences your attribute bonuses:
- Clothes & Light Armor: Boost Substance and Stealth
- Robes: Boost Arcane power
- Heavy Armor: Boost Intimidation
- Daggers: Boost Stealth
- Swords/Heavy/Axes/Claws: Boost Intimidation
- Staves: Boost Arcane
- Bows: Boost Stealth
- Whips/Projectiles/Guns: Boost Substance

\c[6]OPTIMIZATION TIPS:\c[0]
1. Match weapon scaling to your high attributes for maximum damage
2. Build equipment sets that reinforce your preferred scaling type
3. Consider MIX scaling if you have balanced stats
4. Use skills with high-grade scaling when possible
5. Combine equipment types to stack attribute bonuses

Remember: A well-scaled ability hits much harder than a poorly-scaled one. Plan your equipment and skill selection around your scaling strategy!

status_effects.title: Status Effects
status_effects.description:
Various status effects can affect your characters during exploration and combat:
WEATHER-BASED EFFECTS:
- Wet: Applied during rain, may interact with lightning attacks
- Static: Applied during storms, can cause paralysis
- Warm: Applied during snow, helps resist cold damage
COMBAT EFFECTS:
- Poisoned: Gradual HP loss over time
- Paralyzed: Cannot act for several turns
- Stunned: Skip next turn
- Bleeding: HP loss that increases with movement
- Burned: Fire damage over time
- Frozen: Cannot act and take increased damage
SURVIVAL EFFECTS:
- Hungry: Minor stat debuffs when hunger drops below 20%
- Starving: Severe debuffs and HP loss when hunger reaches 0%
- Sleepy: Minor stat debuffs when sleep drops below 20%
- Exhausted: Severe debuffs and MP loss when sleep reaches 0%
MAGICAL EFFECTS:
Various magical spells and abilities can apply positive or negative effects. Read ability descriptions carefully to understand their effects.
MANAGING EFFECTS:
Use items, spells, or rest at safe locations to cure negative status effects. Some effects wear off naturally after time or when battle ends.
Access the Show States menu to see all active status effects on your party members.

stock_market.title: Stock Market
stock_market.description:
The stock market allows you to invest in two types of shares: OIL and SOUL.
ACCESS:
Open the stock market from computers at Hypernet Point or through bankers in major cities.
OIL SHARES:
Highly volatile with prices fluctuating based on complex market forces. High risk, high reward. Good for day-trading if you can predict market movements.
SOUL SHARES:
More stable, with prices primarily influenced by news events. The SOUL Tendency variable controls base price:
- Negative news increases SOUL prices
- Positive news decreases SOUL prices
Prices fluctuate around the tendency value, creating predictable trading opportunities.
INVESTMENT STRATEGY:
- Buy SOUL shares before you expect negative news
- Sell SOUL shares before positive news
- Trade OIL shares based on market timing
- Hold long-term for steady growth
- Diversify your portfolio
INHERITANCE:
Your stock portfolio carries over between characters in Permadeath mode! This makes the stock market one of the few ways to build permanent wealth.
The stock market also affects real estate values and online shopping taxes, so market knowledge benefits you in multiple ways.

travel_&_vehicles.title: Travel & Vehicles
travel_&_vehicles.description:
The game offers multiple transportation methods for navigating the world.
PUBLIC TRANSPORT:
Trains, buses, and other public transit connect major locations. Different transport types have different speeds and costs. Using public transport prevents random enemy encounters.
CAMPER VAN:
Unlocked by starting as Em or Bubba, or completing their quest. The camper:
- Holds 100 liters of fuel
- Consumes 0.5 liters per second while driving
- Contains a fully functional interior with storage
- Can travel anywhere on the world map
- Movement on other maps limited to specific terrain
CAR:
Lighter vehicle that:
- Holds 60 liters of fuel
- Consumes 0.3 liters per second
- Faster than the camper
- Also limited to specific terrain
FUEL SYSTEM:
Both vehicles require fuel to operate. When fuel reaches zero, the vehicle stops completely. Refuel at gas stations found throughout the world.
FAST TRAVEL:
While inside vehicles, you can use fast travel to any discovered location on the world map. The system automatically calculates fuel needed (0.15 liters per distance unit). You can only fast travel if you have enough fuel.
Vehicles are immune to random encounters and can greatly speed up world exploration!

weather_&_time.title: Weather & Time
weather_&_time.description:
The world features a dynamic weather and time system that affects gameplay.
WEATHER TYPES:
- Clear: Normal conditions
- Rain: Applies Wet status, reduces temperature by 3°C
- Storm: Applies Static status, can cause paralysis
- Snow: Applies Warm status, reduces temperature by 8°C
Weather changes based on real-world climate data for different regions. The system uses current season, temperature, and precipitation levels to determine appropriate weather.
TIME OF DAY:
Time cycles through 24 hours, affecting:
- Screen tinting (darker at night, bright at noon)
- Temperature (warmer during afternoon)
- NPC routines (some NPCs only appear at certain times)
DAY/NIGHT CYCLE:
The game calculates realistic sunrise and sunset times for each region. Outdoor areas show the full day/night cycle with changing light levels.
TEMPERATURE EFFECTS:
- Below 5°C: Screen tints blue, characters feel cold
- Above 25°C: Screen tints red/orange, characters feel hot
- Extreme temperatures increase effect intensity
EXTERIOR VS INTERIOR:
- Exterior maps: Full weather and day/night effects
- Interior maps: No weather, only temperature effects
- Covered areas: Like interiors
You can change lighting preferences in the Options menu between Full Cycle, Day Only, Night Only, or Dusk Only.
Weather isn't just cosmetic - it creates strategic opportunities and challenges!

world_map.title: World Map
world_map.description:
The world map (Old Europe) serves as the central hub connecting all locations in the game.
EXPLORATION:
The map shows various points of interest marked with lights. Walk to these locations to discover new areas including:
- Towns and cities
- Dungeons and ruins
- Special locations
- NPC encounters
- Shops and services
AUTO-RETURN:
Many locations have a special tag that automatically returns you to the world map when you reach the map's edge. This makes it easy to explore without getting lost.
TRAVEL METHODS:
From the world map you can:
- Walk to nearby locations
- Use public transport to distant cities
- Fast travel with vehicles (if you have fuel)
- Access the Omega Tower dungeon
SAVING:
The world map is one of the safest places to save your game. Always save before entering dangerous areas!
LOCATION MEMORY:
When you enter a sub-location, the game remembers your position on the world map. When you exit, you'll return to exactly where you entered.
DISCOVERY:
Many secrets are hidden across the world map. Explore thoroughly to find rare items, hidden locations, and unique NPCs. The demo focuses on Italy and the area around Omega Tower, but the full game will expand to cover more of Europe.
The world is dangerous but full of opportunities. Explore carefully!

state_arcane_surge.title: State: Arcane Surge
state_arcane_surge.description:
A powerful magical enhancement that dramatically increases your spell power at the cost of MP drain.
EFFECTS:
- Increases magic attack power by 50%
- Increases magic defense by 50%
- Drains 10% MP per turn
Duration: 3-5 turns
ARCANE SURGE STRATEGY:
This state is perfect for finishing off tough enemies quickly with powerful spells. The MP drain means you need to end fights fast or risk running out of magical energy.
Use Arcane Surge when:
- Facing a single powerful enemy
- You have plenty of MP to spare
- You need to deal maximum magical damage quickly
Be careful in long battles - the MP drain can leave you unable to cast spells!

state_berserk.title: State: Berserk
state_berserk.description:
A state of uncontrollable rage that increases attack power but makes you attack randomly and reduces your defenses.
EFFECTS:
- Increases attack power by 50%
- Decreases defense by 30%
- Forces character to attack random targets
- Cannot be controlled normally
Duration: 2-4 turns
BERSERK RISKS:
When berserked, you lose control of the affected character. They may attack enemies, allies, or even themselves! The increased attack power is tempting, but the lack of control and reduced defense make this a dangerous state.
This state is most dangerous when:
- Your party is low on HP (friendly fire risk)
- Fighting multiple enemies (might attack the wrong one)
- You need strategic positioning
Berserk can be inflicted by certain enemy abilities or items. Use cleansing magic or items to remove it quickly!

state_bleeding.title: State: Bleeding
state_bleeding.description:
A dangerous state where the target continuously loses blood, suffering HP damage over time.
EFFECTS:
- Lose 10% HP per turn
- Does not naturally expire
- Must be healed with items or magic
BLEEDING DANGER:
Unlike poison which expires after a few turns, bleeding persists until treated. This makes it extremely dangerous during long dungeon explorations.
Treatment methods:
- Healing magic
- Bandages or medical items
- Resting at safe locations
- Status cure abilities
Always carry items that can cure bleeding when exploring dangerous areas. Multiple bleeding effects can stack, causing rapid HP loss!

state_blind.title: State: Blind
state_blind.description:
Target's vision is impaired, making it much harder to land attacks.
EFFECTS:
- Reduces hit rate by 50%
- Most physical attacks will miss
- Magic accuracy unaffected
Duration: 3-5 turns, removed at battle end
BLINDNESS TACTICS:
When blinded, switch to magic attacks or abilities that don't require accuracy. Alternatively, use items or abilities that cure blind.
Enemies that blind you:
- Use magic attacks against them
- Have allies attack while you heal
- Use guaranteed-hit abilities if available
Blinding enemies:
- Great for high-attack physical enemies
- Makes dangerous foes much safer
- Combine with evasion buffs for near invincibility

state_burned.title: State: Burned
state_burned.description:
Target is suffering from fire damage, continuously taking burn damage.
EFFECTS:
- Lose 10% HP per turn
- Continues until cured or expires naturally
Duration: Varies, does not end at battle
BURN SYNERGIES:
Burned status interacts with other fire-based effects:
- Fire attacks deal extra damage to burned targets
- Water attacks may cure the burn
- Some abilities specifically target burned enemies
Prevention and cure:
- Fire resistance equipment
- Cold or water-based abilities
- Status cure items
- Natural healing over time
Burned is especially dangerous when combined with other damage-over-time effects like poison or bleeding!

state_charm.title: State: Charm
state_charm.description:
Target is charmed and will attack their own allies!
EFFECTS:
- Forces character to attack their own party
- Cannot be controlled normally
- 50% chance to break when taking damage
Duration: 2-4 turns, removed at battle end
CHARM DANGER:
Charmed characters become enemies temporarily, using their own abilities against their former allies. This is extremely dangerous for powerful party members.
Breaking charm:
- Deal damage to the charmed character
- Use dispel magic
- Wait for the effect to expire
- Use accessories that prevent charm
When fighting enemies that charm:
- Prioritize killing them quickly
- Keep distance from charmed allies
- Have backup healing ready
- Consider charm-prevention equipment

state_cold.title: State: Cold
state_cold.description:
Target is surrounded by a freezing aura that affects their elemental resistances and power.
EFFECTS:
- Weak to Wind attacks (50% extra damage)
- Resistant to Fire attacks (110% power increase)
- Strong against Ice attacks (3x resistance)
- Increased physical attack power (10%)
Duration: 3-5 turns, removed at battle end
COLD STRATEGIES:
The Cold state creates interesting tactical opportunities:
Offensively:
- Your ice attacks become stronger
- Physical attacks deal slightly more damage
Defensively:
- Vulnerable to wind-based attacks
- Near immunity to fire
- Strong defense against ice
This state is often applied by weather conditions or ice-based abilities. Use it to your advantage when planning attacks!

state_confusion.title: State: Confusion
state_confusion.description:
Target is confused and may attack themselves or perform random actions.
EFFECTS:
- 33% chance to attack themselves
- 33% chance to perform random action
- 33% chance to act normally
- May waste important abilities
Duration: 2-4 turns, removed at battle end
CONFUSION CHAOS:
Confusion is unpredictable and can waste valuable turns.
Possible actions when confused:
- Attack self for full damage
- Use items randomly
- Attack random targets
- Waste MP on unnecessary spells
Coping with confusion:
- Use simple commands
- Avoid valuable items
- Position away from allies
- Cure quickly if possible
Confusing enemies:
- Great for disruption
- Wastes their resources
- May cause self-damage
- Breaks their strategy
Confusion is particularly effective against enemies with powerful self-buffs - they might waste them!

state_shadow.title: State: Shadow
state_shadow.description:
Target is surrounded by shadows.
EFFECTS:
- Increases Dark power (25%)
- Increases resistance to Light (50% reduction)
- Vulnerable to Light attacks (25% extra)
- Harder to target
Duration: 5 turns, removed at battle end
SHADOW ADVANTAGE:
Shadow state enhances dark abilities while creating vulnerability to light.
Offensive benefits:
- Dark spells deal more damage
- Shadow abilities enhanced
- Stealth attacks improved
Defensive changes:
- Takes less damage from dark
- Very vulnerable to light
- Harder to hit with physical
Tactical uses:
- Pair with dark magic users
- Avoid light-based enemies
- Use in dark environments
- Combine with evasion
Against shadowed enemies:
- Light attacks for massive damage
- Holy magic is effective
- Illuminate to remove advantage
Shadow state is often found in dark dungeons or applied by shadow creatures!

state_silence.title: State: Silence
state_silence.description:
Target cannot use magic or skills, only basic attacks and items.
EFFECTS:
- Cannot cast spells
- Cannot use magical abilities
- Can still use basic attacks
- Can use items
Duration: 3-5 turns, removed at battle end
SILENCE IMPACT:
Silence is devastating for magic-focused characters but less impactful for physical fighters.
When silenced:
- Use physical attacks instead
- Use items for support
- Have allies provide magic
- Wait for cure
Curing silence:
- Echo Herbs or similar items
- Dispel magic (from allies)
- Wait for expiration
- Status cure abilities
Silencing enemies:
- Shuts down mages completely
- Prevents healing spells
- Stops buff abilities
- Forces physical tactics
Priority targets:
- Enemy healers
- Dangerous mages
- Buffer support enemies
- Magic-dependent bosses
Silence can completely shut down magic-reliant enemies!

state_sleep.title: State: Sleep
state_sleep.description:
Target falls asleep and cannot act, but wakes up when damaged.
EFFECTS:
- Cannot act while asleep
- Completely helpless
- Breaks immediately when hit
- Reduces action speed to zero
Duration: 3-5 turns, breaks when damaged, removed at battle end
SLEEP MECHANICS:
Sleep is powerful crowd control but fragile due to breaking on damage.
Strategic uses:
- Disable dangerous enemies temporarily
- Skip enemy turns safely
- Set up powerful attacks
- Control multiple enemies
Limitations:
- Breaks on ANY damage
- Area attacks wake everyone
- Allies may wake enemies
- Short effective duration
Optimal play:
- Put multiple enemies to sleep
- Kill them one at a time
- Focus sleeping enemies last
- Use sleep to reduce active threats
When asleep:
- Allies must not hit you
- Vulnerable to attacks
- Wakes on damage
- Better than paralysis (can be woken)
Sleep is excellent for controlling combat flow!

state_static.title: State: Static
state_static.description:
Target is charged with electrical energy, affecting their elemental interactions.
EFFECTS:
- Absorbs Lightning damage (heals from it)
- Takes extra Water damage (50%)
- Increases physical attack power (10%)
Duration: 5 turns, removed at battle end
STATIC STATE:
Static creates interesting electrical combat dynamics.
Advantages:
- Lightning attacks heal you
- Slight attack boost
- Can weaponize enemy lightning
Disadvantages:
- Very vulnerable to water
- Risk in wet environments
- Water attacks hurt badly
Synergies:
- Stand in lightning areas
- Allies use lightning on you
- Fight lightning enemies
- Avoid water sources
Against static enemies:
- Water attacks for massive damage
- Never use lightning
- Exploit the weakness
- Combine with Wet status
Static is often applied by weather (storms), electrical attacks, or certain equipment. Very dangerous when combined with Wet status!

state_stun.title: State: Stun
state_stun.description:
Target is stunned and temporarily unable to act.
EFFECTS:
- Cannot act for the duration
- Loses balance and coordination
- Completely disabled
- Reduces action speed to zero
Duration: 1-2 turns, removed at battle end
STUN MECHANICS:
Stun is shorter than paralysis but still completely disabling.
When stunned:
- Cannot do anything
- Must wait it out
- Vulnerable to attacks
- Allies should protect
Curing stun:
- Usually expires quickly
- Status cure items
- Wait for natural recovery
- Ally assistance
Stunning enemies:
- Interrupts their actions
- Prevents dangerous abilities
- Creates openings
- Good for combos
Best uses:
- Interrupt enemy casting
- Stop powerful attacks
- Create burst windows
- Control fast enemies
Stun combos:
- Stun + high damage attack
- Chain stuns on bosses
- Interrupt boss mechanics
- Prevent healing
The short duration makes stun perfect for tactical interruption rather than long-term disable!

state_unbalanced.title: State: Unbalanced
state_unbalanced.description:
Target loses balance and may fail actions at the end of each turn.
EFFECTS:
- Chance to fail actions
- Reduced hit rate by 30%
- 50% chance to break when damaged
- Unreliable actions
Duration: 1-3 turns, removed at battle end or when damaged
UNBALANCED EFFECTS:
Unbalanced makes the target unreliable, potentially wasting their turns.
Risks:
- Actions may fail randomly
- Attacks miss more often
- Wasted turns
- Unpredictable behavior
When unbalanced:
- Use simpler actions
- Focus on guaranteed effects
- Prepare for failures
- Consider waiting
Curing unbalanced:
- Stance abilities
- Balance restoration items
- Take damage (50% chance to break)
- Wait for expiration
Inflicting unbalanced:
- Disrupts enemy strategies
- Makes them unreliable
- Wastes their turns
- Good against precision enemies
Unbalanced is particularly effective against enemies that rely on complex ability chains!

state_vulnerability.title: State: Vulnerability
state_vulnerability.description:
Target is cursed with random elemental weaknesses that change each turn.
EFFECTS:
- Random elemental weakness each turn
- Unpredictable vulnerabilities
- Reduces defense by 50%
- Cannot be predicted
Duration: Persists until cured, doesn't naturally expire
VULNERABILITY CURSE:
This curse makes the target unpredictably weak to different elements.
Dangers:
- Never know which element is weak
- Could be weak to anything
- Greatly reduced defense
- Persists indefinitely
Coping strategies:
- Cure immediately if possible
- Assume worst-case scenario
- Avoid elemental attacks from enemies
- Use physical defense
Exploiting on enemies:
- Try different elements
- Usually find a weakness
- Combine with multi-element attacks
- Guaranteed to have some weakness
Removal:
- Curse removal items
- Dispel magic
- Divine cleansing
- Powerful healing
Vulnerability is especially dangerous against enemies with varied elemental attacks!

state_wet.title: State: Wet
state_wet.description:
Target is soaking wet, changing their elemental resistances.
EFFECTS:
- Absorbs Water damage (heals from water)
- Reduced Fire damage (50% less)
- Takes DOUBLE Lightning damage (200%)
Duration: 5 turns, removed at battle end
WET STATE DANGERS:
Being wet makes you extremely vulnerable to lightning attacks.
Major risk:
- Lightning deals double damage
- One lightning attack can kill
- Thunder spells are lethal
Minor benefits:
- Fire barely hurts
- Water heals you
- Good in fire areas
When wet:
- AVOID LIGHTNING
- Stay away from electrical enemies
- Use fire resistance less
- Be cautious in storms
Against wet enemies:
- Lightning for massive damage
- Thunder magic is devastating
- Avoid fire (not effective)
- Never use water (heals them)
Weather effects:
- Rain applies Wet status
- Standing in water
- Water-based attacks
Wet + Static = Devastating combination for enemies to exploit!

petro.title: Petro
petro.description:
Target is soaking wet, changing their elemental resistances.
EFFECTS:
- Absorbs Water damage (heals from water)
- Reduced Fire damage (50% less)
- Takes DOUBLE Lightning damage (200%)
Duration: 5 turns, removed at battle end
WET STATE DANGERS:
Being wet makes you extremely vulnerable to lightning attacks.
Major risk:
- Lightning deals double damage
- One lightning attack can kill
- Thunder spells are lethal
Minor benefits:
- Fire barely hurts
- Water heals you
- Good in fire areas
When wet:
- AVOID LIGHTNING
- Stay away from electrical enemies
- Use fire resistance less
- Be cautious in storms
Against wet enemies:
- Lightning for massive damage
- Thunder magic is devastating
- Avoid fire (not effective)
- Never use water (heals them)
Weather effects:
- Rain applies Wet status
- Standing in water
- Water-based attacks
Wet + Static = Devastating combination for enemies to exploit!
